generated_oml:
// C3: Physical Sensing Components
instance LiDARPointCloudSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "LiDAR sensor providing raw point cloud data for object detection and tracking in autonomous driving scenarios"
    DTDFVocab:producedData LiDARRawPointCloudData
]

instance VehicleStateSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Sensor suite collecting telemetry including motor actuation, steering servo signals, and vehicle dynamics"
    DTDFVocab:producedData VehicleStateTelemetryData
]

instance MotorActuationSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Sensor monitoring motor actuation commands and execution status for adaptive cruise control"
    DTDFVocab:producedData MotorActuationFeedbackData
]

instance SteeringServoSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Sensor tracking steering servo signals and adjustments for vehicle path control"
    DTDFVocab:producedData SteeringServoFeedbackData
]

// C4: Physical-to-Virtual Interaction
instance LiDARRawPointCloudData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Unstructured point cloud data transmitted from LiDAR sensor as individual points on a 2D plane"
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom LiDARPointCloudSensor
]

instance VehicleStateTelemetryData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Processed vehicle state data including motor actuation, steering servo signals, and vehicle dynamics"
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom VehicleStateSensor
]

instance MotorActuationFeedbackData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Actuation commands and execution status from motor control system"
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom MotorActuationSensor
]

instance SteeringServoFeedbackData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Steering servo signals and adjustments for vehicle path control"
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom SteeringServoSensor
]

instance PointCloudClusteringData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Structured object tracks and spatial clusters derived from raw LiDAR data via Point Cloud Clustering & Tracking component"
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance PointCloudClusteringModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Model for converting raw LiDAR point clouds into structured clusters and tracks for object detection"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ROS2Middleware
]

instance VehicleStateModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Digital representation of vehicle state including dynamics, sensor fusion, and control parameters"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ROS2Middleware
]

instance AdaptiveCruiseControlModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Model-based design of ACC algorithms for computing desired speed based on lead vehicle distance"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo MATLABSimulinkEnvironment
]

instance LiDARRawPointCloudDataStore : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Raw LiDAR point cloud data streamed from physical twin to digital twin"
    DTDFVocab:fromData LiDARRawPointCloudData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ROS2Middleware
]

instance ProcessedVehicleStateData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Processed vehicle state data including motor actuation, steering servo signals, and vehicle dynamics"
    DTDFVocab:fromData VehicleStateTelemetryData, MotorActuationFeedbackData, SteeringServoFeedbackData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ROS2Middleware
]

instance ObjectTrackData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Structured object tracks and spatial clusters derived from raw LiDAR data"
    DTDFVocab:fromData PointCloudClusteringData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ROS2Middleware
]

instance DesiredSpeedData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Computed desired speed based on distance to lead vehicle for adaptive cruise control"
    DTDFVocab:fromData ObjectTrackData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ROS2Middleware
]

instance VehicleStateDatabase : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Structured storage of vehicle state data, sensor logs, and user-generated reports for long-term analysis"
    DTDFVocab:fromData ProcessedVehicleStateData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SQLDatabase
]

// C11: Enablers
instance ROS2Middleware : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Primary middleware framework for inter-process communication, sensor data fusion, and actuator control with real-time capabilities and modularity"
    DTDFVocab:enables DataMonitoringService, ComputationOffloadingService, RemoteManagementService
]

instance MATLABSimulinkEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Model-based design environment for ACC algorithms with co-simulation capabilities for rapid prototyping and validation"
    DTDFVocab:enables ComputationOffloadingService
]

instance SQLDatabase : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Structured database for storing vehicle state data, sensor logs, and user-generated reports with support for querying and historical trend analysis"
    DTDFVocab:enables DataMonitoringService
]

instance WiFiCommunication : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Low-latency bidirectional wireless communication infrastructure for real-time data streaming between physical twin and digital twin"
    DTDFVocab:enables DataMonitoringService, ComputationOffloadingService, RemoteManagementService
]

instance DataTranslationLayer : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Middleware component ensuring serialization alignment, message type standardization, and unit conversions for all data streams"
    DTDFVocab:enables DataMonitoringService
]

instance PointCloudClusteringComponent : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Software component for converting raw LiDAR data into structured clusters and tracks via noise filtering and spatial processing"
    DTDFVocab:enables DataMonitoringService
]

// C6: Services
instance DataMonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service providing real-time display of vehicle state data, visualization of projected driving path and detected object tracks, and user interface for data management"
    DTDFVocab:provides VehicleStateVisualizationInsight, DrivingPathProjectionInsight, ObjectTrackVisualizationInsight, DataReportingAction
]

instance ComputationOffloadingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service offloading ACC system computation from resource-constrained ECUs to digital twin, computing desired speed based on lead vehicle distance"
    DTDFVocab:provides DesiredSpeedCalculationInsight, ComputationalOffloadingAction
]

instance RemoteManagementService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service enabling remote enabling/disabling of ACC functionality, setting desired speed, sending emergency braking commands, and real-time state monitoring"
    DTDFVocab:provides RemoteControlInsight, EmergencyBrakingAction, ACCStatusMonitoringInsight
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance VehicleStateVisualizationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Real-time display of vehicle state data including speed, position, and dynamics"
]

instance DrivingPathProjectionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Visualization of projected driving path based on current vehicle state and control inputs"
]

instance ObjectTrackVisualizationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Real-time visualization of detected object tracks in proximity to the vehicle"
]

instance DataReportingAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "User interface functionality for viewing, saving, and generating reports of vehicle state data"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance DesiredSpeedCalculationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Computed desired speed based on distance to lead vehicle for adaptive cruise control"
]

instance ComputationalOffloadingAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Offloading ACC computation from vehicle ECUs to digital twin for resource management"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance RemoteControlInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Real-time state data display for operational oversight and remote management"
]

instance EmergencyBrakingAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Remote transmission of emergency braking commands to the physical vehicle"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance ACCStatusMonitoringInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Monitoring of ACC system status including enable/disable state and operational parameters"
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "
The **virtual-to-physical interaction** in this Digital Twin System (DTS) involves the transmission of control commands from the **Digital Twin Entity** to the **Physical Twin Entity**, governed by the following technical specifications:

1. **Command Types**:
   - **Actuation Commands**: Motor and servo control signals (e.g., steering adjustments, throttle modulation) generated by the *Actuation FE* component.
   - **Adaptive Control Commands**: Dynamic adjustments (e.g., Adaptive Cruise Control, ACC) derived from the *Controlling FE* component, which processes input data (e.g., vehicle state, object tracks) to compute optimal control actions.

2. **Control Message Formats**:
   - **Standardized via Data Translation**: Commands are serialized and aligned with message types using the *Data Translation* component, ensuring compatibility across the system.
   - **ROS2 Message Types**: Control signals are likely encapsulated in ROS2 message formats for intra-entity communication before being translated for inter-entity transmission.

3. **Validation and Authentication Mechanisms**:
   - **Not Explicitly Documented**: The provided documents do not specify cryptographic authentication or validation protocols for control commands. However, the use of **UDP over Wi-Fi** implies potential vulnerabilities (e.g., spoofing, replay attacks) that may require external safeguards (e.g., IPsec, digital signatures) not mentioned here.

4. **Feedback Systems**:
   - **Actuation Feedback Loop**: The *Actuation FE* component receives execution confirmation from the Physical Twin Entity (e.g., motor/servo response status), enabling closed-loop control.
   - **Real-Time Monitoring**: The *Data Monitoring* service visualizes control outcomes (e.g., projected driving path, object tracks) in the 3D visualization (Fig. 3a), allowing operators to validate command efficacy.

5. **Conflict Resolution**:
   - **Not Explicitly Documented**: No mechanisms (e.g., priority arbitration, deadlock prevention) are described for resolving conflicts between concurrent control commands (e.g., manual vs. automated steering overrides).

6. **Command Execution Confirmation**:
   - **Implicit via Actuation FE**: The *Actuation FE* component likely validates command execution by comparing intended vs. actual actuator states (e.g., servo position feedback), though specifics are not detailed in the document.
"
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin System (DTS) enforces a **100 ms time synchronization accuracy** between physical and digital components to enable computation offloading and coordination, adhering to hardware limitations. This temporal granularity is critical for safety-critical vehicular applications, where stricter synchronization (e.g., sub-100 ms) is mandated by many standards but not implemented here. The system does not explicitly specify varying time scales for different services, but the **ROS2-based architecture** (with each Functionality Entity (FE) implemented as an individual ROS2 package) implies real-time or near-real-time processing constraints for sensor-actuator loops, data pre-processing, and device communication. Latency requirements are implicitly tied to the **Physical Twin Entity**, which hosts resource-specific functionality like *Point Cloud Clustering & Tracking*, suggesting low-latency demands for real-time environmental perception. No explicit mention of hierarchical time scales or asynchronous coordination mechanisms is provided beyond the 100 ms synchronization baseline."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The DTS orchestrates its constellation through a **hybrid architecture pattern** combining:
  - **ISO 23247 RA compliance** for high-level entity structuring (User, Digital Twin, Physical Twin Entities), with deviations to accommodate general automotive functionality (e.g., *Point Cloud Clustering*) in the *Physical Twin Entity*.
  - **ROS2-based component integration**, where each FE is implemented as an independent ROS2 package (e.g., *Data Storage* as a standalone component despite lacking a direct ISO 23247 FE alignment). This enables modularity and dynamic reconfiguration but requires explicit **inter-package communication** (ROS2 topics/services) for coordination.
  - **Resource management** tied to hardware deployment: entities are mapped to distinct hardware components, with the *Physical Twin Entity* hosting resource-critical functions (sensors/actuators) and the *Digital Twin Entity* managing control/data flows. The architecture lacks explicit **global resource orchestration** (e.g., Kubernetes-like scheduling) but relies on **hardware-level synchronization** (100 ms clock) and ROS2’s real-time capabilities for temporal coordination.
  - **System-wide coordination mechanisms** are implicit via ROS2’s publish-subscribe model and the **entity-based partitioning**, though no centralized controller or federated learning is mentioned. The *User Interface FE* split (visualization/control) suggests a **user-driven orchestration layer** for human-in-the-loop operations."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin System (DTS) mandates **clock synchronization** with an accuracy of **100 ms** across all components to ensure **data integrity** and **event ordering**, particularly critical in **distributed systems**. This synchronization supports coordinated operations and prevents inconsistencies in real-time data exchange. No explicit validation/verification methods, uncertainty quantification, or error correction mechanisms are detailed in the provided documents, though the requirement implies reliance on **time-stamped data** and **synchronized protocols** for fidelity assurance."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "
The **technical network infrastructure** of this Digital Twin System (DTS) is designed with the following specifications:

1. **Communication Protocols**:
   - **Intra-Entity (Software Components)**: **ROS2 (Robot Operating System 2)** is used for real-time communication between software components within the Digital Twin Entity. ROS2 provides topic-based messaging, service calls, and parameter servers for modular and scalable interactions.
   - **Inter-Entity (Physical-Digital)**: **Wi-Fi + UDP (User Datagram Protocol)** is employed for low-latency, high-throughput data exchange between the Physical Twin Entity and Digital Twin Entity. UDP is preferred over TCP for its reduced overhead, though it lacks built-in reliability guarantees.

2. **Network Topology and Architecture**:
   - **Distributed Architecture**: The system follows a **hybrid topology** where:
     - **Physical Twin Entity**: Sensors (LiDAR, motor, steering, servo) transmit raw data to the Digital Twin Entity.
     - **Digital Twin Entity**: Hosts ROS2-based software components (e.g., *Point Cloud Clustering & Tracking*, *Controlling FE*) that process and visualize data.
     - **User Entity**: Interfaces with the Digital Twin via a **3D Visualization User Interface** (e.g., virtual cockpit, Fig. 3).
   - **Data Flow**: Sensor data propagates from the Physical Twin → Digital Twin → User Entity, with control commands flowing in the reverse direction (Fig. 2).

3. **Bandwidth and Latency Requirements**:
   - **Not Quantified**: The document does not specify exact bandwidth (e.g., Mbps) or latency (e.g., ms) targets. However, the use of **real-time ROS2** and **UDP** implies critical paths (e.g., LiDAR data, actuation commands) require **sub-100ms latency** for responsive control.
   - **LiDAR Data**: Point cloud streams (high-resolution, high-frequency) likely demand significant bandwidth, necessitating efficient preprocessing (e.g., clustering) to reduce payload size.

4. **Reliability and Redundancy Mechanisms**:
   - **Not Explicitly Documented**: The system does not describe redundancy (e.g., backup sensors, failover nodes) or reliability protocols (e.g., retransmission for UDP). However, the *Data Translation* component may include error-checking for message integrity.
   - **ROS2 Resilience**: ROS2 supports **QoS (Quality of Service) policies** for critical data streams, though specifics are not provided.

5. **Security Protocols**:
   - **Not Explicitly Documented**: No encryption (e.g., TLS), access control, or intrusion detection mechanisms are mentioned for the Wi-Fi/UDP or ROS2 communication layers. Potential risks include:
     - **UDP Vulnerabilities**: Packet loss or spoofing without safeguards.
     - **ROS2 Exposure**: Unauthenticated topic access could enable malicious command injection.
   - **Implied Safeguards**: The *Data Translation* component may enforce message validation, but this is not detailed.
"
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "
The **deployment infrastructure** for this Digital Twin System (DTS) is characterized by the following technical and operational aspects:

1. **Hosting Platforms**:
   - **Not Explicitly Documented**: The document does not specify whether the Digital Twin Entity is hosted on **cloud**, **edge**, or **on-premise** platforms. However, the use of **ROS2** and **Wi-Fi/UDP** suggests:
     - **Edge Deployment**: Likely co-located with the Physical Twin Entity (e.g., onboard a vehicle) to minimize latency for real-time control.
     - **On-Premise**: The *3D Visualization User Interface* (Fig. 3) implies a local deployment for low-latency operator interaction.

2. **Computational and Storage Requirements**:
   - **Computational**:
     - **ROS2 Network**: Requires sufficient CPU/GPU resources to handle real-time processing of LiDAR point clouds, clustering, and control logic.
     - **Point Cloud Processing**: The *Point Cloud Clustering & Tracking* component demands significant computational power for 3D data analysis.
   - **Storage**:
     - **Data Storage FE**: Stores processed data (e.g., reports, object tracks) for historical analysis or compliance. No capacity details are provided.
     - **Reporting FE**: Generates reports from stored data, implying persistent storage for logs and visualizations.

3. **Scalability Mechanisms**:
   - **Not Explicitly Documented**: The document does not describe horizontal/vertical scaling strategies (e.g., containerization, microservices) or dynamic resource allocation for handling multiple vehicles or sensors.
   - **ROS2 Scalability**: ROS2 supports distributed architectures, but scalability limits depend on network bandwidth and computational resources.

4. **Deployment Models Supported**:
   - **Single-Instance Deployment**: The architecture (Fig. 2) suggests a **monolithic or tightly coupled** deployment where components are co-located or tightly integrated within a single entity (e.g., vehicle).
   - **Modular Extensibility**: ROS2’s modular design allows for future expansion (e.g., adding new sensors or control algorithms) without redesigning the core system.

5. **Availability and Reliability Assurance**:
   - **Not Explicitly Documented**: No SLAs (Service Level Agreements), uptime guarantees, or redundancy strategies (e.g., hot standby) are mentioned. However:
     - **Real-Time Constraints**: The system’s reliance on real-time data (e.g., LiDAR, actuation) implies high availability requirements.
     - **Data Translation Redundancy**: The *Data Translation* component may include fallback mechanisms for message serialization, though specifics are absent.
"
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system adheres to the **ISO 23247 – Digital twin framework for manufacturing**, specifically **Part 2: Reference Architecture**, which defines:
  - An **entity-based architecture** with standardized roles for **Observable Manufacturing Elements (OMEs)**, **Data Collection/Control Entities**, **Digital Twin Entities**, and **User Entities**.
  - **Interoperability standards** are implied via **modular Functional Entities (FEs)**, though no explicit protocols (e.g., OPC UA, MTConnect) are mandated beyond the example of **OPC Unified Architecture** for data exchange.
  - **Compliance frameworks** are not detailed, but the standard ensures **modularity** and **scalability** for manufacturing DTs. **Certification requirements** are not mentioned in the provided context, nor are **domain-specific standards** (e.g., IEC 62264 for enterprise-control systems integration). The architecture focuses on **structural standardization** rather than prescriptive compliance or certification processes."
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system (DTS) for Adaptive Cruise Control (ACC) adheres to safety-critical requirements as outlined in **ISO 15622:2018**, which specifies performance and test procedures for ACC systems. The system integrates **Vehicle-to-Vehicle (V2V) communication** based on **3GPP TS 22.185 (Release 15)**, ensuring compliance with service requirements for latency-sensitive V2V interactions. While the documents emphasize the **safety-critical nature of the system**, they do not explicitly detail cybersecurity protocols (e.g., authentication, encryption, or digital signatures), access control mechanisms, or threat mitigation strategies beyond the referenced standards. The DTS is designed to implement ACC functionalities as per **ISO 15622**, but no granular specifications for **ISO 26262-1:2018** (functional safety) or **HARA/MDA** (Hazard Analysis and Risk Assessment / Model-Driven Architecture) are provided in the given context."
]
